key_insight: A hybrid GNN-LSTM surrogate can learn the static stress-strain response of additively manufactured short-fiber thermoplastics from microstructure topology and loading history.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast, physics-informed screening of weak microstructural regions in SFT parts, supporting digital twins and design optimization for lightweight aerospace and automotive components.
method: Reconstruct microstructures from micro-CT and discretize them into Voronoi-based interaction cells.
Run nonlinear FE homogenization per cell, including matrix damage, to generate stress-strain training data.
Train a GNN-LSTM to encode microstructural connectivity plus path-dependent mechanical evolution.
evidence_signal: The surrogate matches high-fidelity FE predictions with about R^2 ≈ 0.98 while reducing computation by more than two orders of magnitude.
watch_item: Performance depends on calibrated damage laws and representative microstructure data, so extrapolation to unseen manufacturing conditions may be unreliable.
paper_title: On Surrogate Modeling of Static Response of AM Short-Fiber Thermoplastics Using Graph Neural Networks
